meaning: n.[动物]大羚羊(非洲产,最大的羚羊)
examples: The common eland is pale brown, becoming blue-gray with age, and often marked with narrow, vertical white stripes.
The large and very heavy giant eland of forests and plains in western Africa looks more like an ox than an antelope.
Mashatu reserve has an impressive population of African elephant, eland and ostrich. the baobab trees are also plenty in the region.
The menu changes daily to reflect the produce in season, but it often features game, such as eland and wildebeest.
The plantation's menagerie also includes rhinoceroses, the ostrichlike cassowary, cheetahs and giant eland, a type of large African antelope.
